events.headline: Mauritius weather bulletin – 25 March 2026
events.summary: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 11h30 on Wednesday 25 March 2026.General situation:A light and warm airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Clouds development are expected this afternoon over the Western part of the island and over the Central plateau with localised showers.These showers may be sometimes of moderate intensity with risks of thunderstorm and may spread in other sectors late in the afternoon.The maximum temperature will be above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.The sky will clear up gradually in the evening and will be mainly fair across the island during the night and tomorrow morning.The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind from the Eastern sector at about 15 km/h, becoming variable during the night.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :22h11 and tomorrow 22h57.Low tides :12h28 and tomorrow 15h37.Sunset :18h17.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.

events.headline: Agriterra mise sur la canne à sucre pour développer un écosystème durable
events.headline_original: Agriterra mise sur la canne à sucre pour développer un écosystème durable
events.summary: Agriterra, le pôle agriculture du groupe Terra, met en avant un modèle basé sur l’économie circulaire, où chaque composant de la canne à sucre est valorisé pour réduire les déchets et produire des ressources durables. Dans un contexte marqué par la recherche d’alternatives aux énergies fossiles, l’entreprise souligne que la canne à sucre peut être exploitée dans son intégralité. La bagasse et la paille sont transformées en énergie renouvelable, la mélasse est utilisée pour produire de l’alcool i

events.headline: En solidarité à Paul Bérenger : Iqbal Calcateea met fin à son mandat au MSC

Et ce, afin d’apporter son soutien à Paul Bérenger, récemment démissionnaire de son poste de vice-Premier ministre. events.headline: Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, nouveau capitaine de la Géorgie
events.headline_original: Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, nouveau capitaine de la Géorgie
events.summary: Absente de la Coupe du Monde, malgré un Euro 2024 séduisant, la Géorgie a un nouveau capitaine. Il s’agit de la star de la sélection, Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (45 sélections, 20 buts), présente en équipe nationale depuis 2019. Il prend la suite de Guram Kashia (38 ans), défenseur du Slovan Bratislava. « À partir de juin 2026, l’équipe nationale géorgienne sera menée par Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, Otar Kiteishvili sera le premier vice-capitaine et Otar Kakabadze le second », a annoncé la Fédération Géor

events.headline: US to deploy elite army brigade to Middle East: reports
events.summary: [France 24] The United States is planning to send some 3,000 soldiers from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East to support operations against Iran, US media reported Tuesday. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the Middle East by Friday, the latest deadline set by President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal. The Guardian Washington bureau chief David Smith reports. [Al Jazeera] The US is expected to deploy up to 3,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East.

events.headline: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
events.summary: [BBC News] Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured. [France 24] Moscow launched nearly 1,000 drones at Ukraine within 24 hours in one of its biggest-ever daytime attacks, killing at least eight people and damaging a UNESCO heritage site in Lviv. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday slammed Moscow's "absolute depravity" and said Russia "has no intention of actually ending this war".

events.headline: Iran war pushes plastic packaging costs higher
events.summary: Oil prices rose again on Tuesday, a day after a sharp decline, amid confusion over which way the Iran war was going. Brent crude settled well above 100 dollars a barrel. Meanwhile, rising energy costs stemming from the conflict are causing the cost of plastic packaging to skyrocket, which in turn will make everything on supermarket shelves more expensive. But first, the EU and Australia sealed a landmark free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on most goods.

events.headline: Ghana signs security partnership with the EU
events.summary: In tonight's edition, Ghana has become the first African country to enter into a landmark security and defence partnership with the European Union. Also, with several African countries heavily reliant on fertiliser imported from the Gulf, there's been a knock-on effect on the continent as the war in Iran chokes traditional supply routes. And the death toll from heavy flooding in Kenya has reached 88, as at least 21 counties are affected.

events.headline: 'We need a more European NATO': Rasmussen warns the US is 'turning its back on Europe'

According to NATO's Secretary General, Europe is entering a decisive strategic rupture, one that forces a long-delayed reckoning with its own dependency structures and security assumptions. The erosion of American commitment to European defence is not a hypothetical risk but an unfolding reality, exposing the fragility of a model built on external guarantees and internal complacency.

events.headline: War on Iran: 'Fought on faulty assumptions, notion of externally delivered regime change delusional'
events.summary: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Barbara Slavin, Author and Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center. According to Slavin, the war on Iran is not a coherent strategic endeavour, and reflects a pattern in which rhetoric trumps reality. Rather than emerging from necessity, diplomatic alternatives were dismissed and intelligence warnings inadequately considered. Slavin warns military actions have strengthened hardline elements within the Iranian regime and has destabilised the global economy.

events.headline: French economic growth undercut by war in Iran, national statistics office says
events.summary: The French economy will grow by only 0.2% in the first half of the year instead of the 0.3% forecast, the Insee national statistics agency said Tuesday, citing higher inflation linked to the war in Iran. Inflation will surpass 2% in coming months, rising sharply from the 1.1% rate seen in February.

events.headline: littlefish raises $9.5 million to scale its merchant operating system across Africa
events.summary: littlefish, the South African startup building financial infrastructure for small businesses, has raised a $9.5 million Series A round to scale its merchant operating system and expand across Africa.
